daily.baseball_pulse:
Nico Hoerner locked in a massive contract extension with the Cubs, signing a six-year, $141 million deal that ties him to the team through 2030. This is the fourth-largest contract ever for a second baseman and represents a long-term commitment to one of the league's most versatile players. Deyvison De Los Santos is being recalled by the Marlins for his MLB debut, giving you a speculative add opportunity if you need depth at catcher. The Orioles are finalizing an extension with Shane Baz while simultaneously working on their offseason acquisitions. Meanwhile, several prospects are signing minor league deals as organizational depth, including Peter Lambert with the Astros, Andrew Chafin with the Reds, and multiple Angels signings including Chris Taylor and Hunter Strickland.

Kevin McGonigle is making an immediate fantasy impact in his Tigers debut, going 5-for-8 with 4 RBI across his first two games, including a clutch 10-pitch at-bat that resulted in a go-ahead two-run single. The second overall prospect is already demonstrating the contact skills and situational hitting that made him a top prospect. Robert Gasser struck out 11 of 17 batters in a scoreless Triple-A debut for the Brewers, showing dominant stuff in his season opener. Chase Petty delivered eight strikeouts over 4 and two-thirds scoreless innings in his Triple-A debut for the Reds with an 18 whiff rate on 35 swings. Konnor Griffin, the number one overall prospect, reached base twice and stole a base in his Triple-A debut, keeping his path to the majors on an accelerated timeline. Chase DeLauter has been absolutely sensational early, following a two-homer regular season debut with another home run in his second game, and has now hit three homers in his first six at-bats of the season.

Emmet Sheehan's fastball velocity showed a concerning decline during his outing, dropping from 95.7 mph to 91.2 mph by his final pitch, a 4.5 mph decrease that suggests potential fatigue or mechanical issues requiring fantasy monitoring going forward.

Opening Day brought excellent early-season performances from established stars and improving depth pieces alike. Mike Trout is off to an exceptional start, joining Amos Otis as the only player since 1900 to homer and reach base four times in his team's first two games.
